text:
Once in a sleepy town filled with autumn-colored leaves, there lived a wise old owl named Olaf. He didn't say much, but everyone knew Olaf held the secrets of the forest.

On a chilly morning, Olaf listened to the soft hum of birds and insects. Feeling a pang of loneliness, he decided to visit Martha, a cheerful hedgehog who lived near his favorite oak tree.

Martha waved warmly from her cozy nest. "Oh dear Olaf, you're looking quite somber today. What troubles you?"

Olaf let out a hoot and spoke earnestly, "I've lost my way. Every day I wander farther than I mean to."

Just then, a robin perched on a branch. "You wish, Olaf?"

"Yes. I long for companionship."

The robin fluttered closer. "I was once just a chick. I've seen many moons."

Olaf's eyes twinkled. "Tell me a story."
